events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need assess scenario: Spool erection & fit-up, heavy industrial, Piping & Plumbing. Observed field condition: flange bolt-hole rotation wrong (two-holing missed). Work roughed in. Reference ASME B31.3, project iso drawings. Treat as roughly 70 percent complete until defects corrected.

Need decide decision: fail because non-compliant defect. Risk? Flange bolt-hole rotation wrong (two-holing missed) can cause misalignment, gasket compression issues, leaks, especially in piping. In industrial piping, two-holing is required for proper alignment and bolt installation. Risk likely high? Could be medium or high. For a flange bolt-hole rotation wrong, it's a fit-up defect; not immediately dangerous but can lead to leak under pressure. In heavy industrial, high consequence. Risk high. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: 70 until defects corrected, maybe 70. Value not asked, 0. Sound_source etc default empty? Schema says string for sound_source, numbers. We can set sound_source: "", event_time:0, rate:0, order empty list, workable empty list. Findings: "Flange bolt-hole rotation does not match drawing (two-holing missed)", "Spool is misaligned/non-compliant with project specifications". Actions: "Loosen and re-position flange/spool to correct bolt-hole orientation per iso drawing", "Re-torque bolts to spec after alignment", "Re-inspect fit-up before hydro/test". Rationale: "Two-holing missed violates project iso drawings and ASME B31.3 alignment requirements; flange must be rotated to correct orientation and re-bolted before close-out." References: "ASME B31.3", "project iso drawings". Confidence high 0.95.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  ],

The spool is physically installed but defective and non-compliant, so it must be re-fit before the work can progress to test/inspection or acceptance.",
